summary: Improvement cycle addressing sourcery-ai[bot] external review #1. Three concerns were raised: (1) confusing REQ-007/REQ-008 numbering after dropping the replicas-zero case, (2) duplicated minimal-valid Keystone scaffold across the 10 CC-0094 invalid-CR fixtures, (3) lowercase 'chainsaw' typo in keystone-crd.md:1165. All three are resolved: a fenced 'Numbering gap (CC-0094 review-1)' comment block was added in chainsaw-test.yaml between REQ-006 and REQ-008 explaining why REQ-007 / 08-replicas-zero.yaml is intentionally absent (mutating webhook coerces replicas:0 → 3 before Minimum=1 fires); a single-source-of-truth Python generator tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/_generate.py was added that emits all 10 CC-0094 fixtures from a canonical scaffold and supports a `--check` drift mode; the typo was applied verbatim. Supporting doc updates were added to docs/reference/keystone-crd.md (regen paragraph) and docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md (file tree + 'generated' tags). Three review-pattern files captured for future enforcement. Drift-check (`_generate.py --check`) returns 0 against the on-disk fixtures. No production code (operators/**, internal/**, deploy/**) touched. Pre-review validation (ruff, mypy, radon, vulture, stubs) all pass on _generate.py.

tests_checklist.item: Drift check (_generate.py --check) passes against on-disk fixtures
All 10 CC-0094 fixtures still resolve to existing files referenced by chainsaw-test.yaml
metadata.name is unique across all 12 invalid-cr manifests
CC-0012 fixtures (00-, 01-) intentionally NOT regenerated and preserved verbatim
Numbering-gap comment block explains REQ-007 / 08-replicas-zero.yaml absence
Generator drift-check actually wired into CI (Makefile / workflow)

code_quality_checklist.item: Generator follows existing python-script conventions (SPDX header, docstring, dataclass)
No code duplication — single canonical scaffold for all 10 fixtures

security_checklist.item: Generator only writes to its own directory (Path(__file__).resolve().parent)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible (string-templating generator vs. full YAML library)
Tests-and-docs only — no scope creep into operator code

dry_yagni_checklist.item: 10 fixtures now derive from one canonical scaffold (resolves duplication concern)
No speculative features in generator (only emits what the suite needs today)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Drift detected by --check exits non-zero with explicit filename list
Numbering gap is documented at the point of confusion (chainsaw-test.yaml line 112-124)

defensive_checklist.item: Generator validates rendered output against existing on-disk content before overwriting (idempotent)
--check mode does not modify files; only reports drift

issues_found.description: The chainsaw-test.yaml header comment and docs/reference/keystone-crd.md:1222 both state "CI may invoke `_generate.py --check`" but the drift check is NOT wired into Makefile or any .github/workflows/ file. Without CI enforcement, the canonical-scaffold guarantee that motivated the generator (sourcery-ai concern #2) is only enforced by reviewer discipline. A hand-edit to any of 02-/03-/.../12-*.yaml would land silently.
issues_found.fix: Add a `lint-fixtures` (or similar) target to Makefile that runs `python3 tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/_generate.py --check`, and reference it from the existing lint job in .github/workflows/ci.yaml. Alternatively, add a pre-commit hook entry. Then change the chainsaw-test.yaml header from "CI may invoke" to "CI invokes" so the comment matches reality.
issues_found.evidence: grep for `_generate.py --check` and `invalid-cr/_generate` across Makefile and .github/workflows/ returned no matches.

suggested_improvements: Wire `_generate.py --check` into CI/Makefile so drift fails the build instead of relying on the aspirational `CI may invoke` comment.

Consider adding a one-line unit test that imports _generate.py and asserts len(FIXTURES) == 10 and that every Fixture.filename matches the pattern listed in chainsaw-test.yaml — catches accidental fixture removal/rename in a fast unit-test layer rather than waiting for chainsaw to fail.
next_steps: Optional: wire the drift check into CI as described under issues_found / FC1.

Run live-cluster verification (task 4.2 from the plan): `make kind-up && chainsaw test tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr` on two consecutive parallel-4 runs. Sandbox cannot execute this — honest blocker per memory project_sandbox_no_docker.md.
